bibliographic_data.title: Fast Discovery of Nested Dependencies on JSON Data
bibliographic_data.abstract: Functional and inclusion dependencies are the most widely used classes of data dependencies in data profiling due to their ability to identify relationships in data such as primary and foreign keys. These relationships are equally important when dealing with nested data formats such as JSON. However, the definition of functional and inclusion dependencies makes use of a flat, unnested relational model which misses many useful types of dependencies on data which involve nested data models. In this work, we identify types of dependencies which are not captured by traditional functional and inclusion dependencies but which nevertheless capture meaningful relationships among nested data. We also demonstrate how algorithms for mining these traditional dependencies can be adapted to also mine nested dependencies. The first strategy simply flattens the input data and feeds into unmodified existing algorithms. We present a second strategy which instead adapts the algorithm to efficiently process JSON data as input which in some cases leads to a reduction in runtime by multiple orders of magnitude on real-world datasets. We further show how these algorithms can be adapted to produce useful results in the presence of a percentage of incomplete or invalid data.

core_content.novelty_claim: The work extends traditional functional and inclusion dependencies to nested JSON data, providing efficient algorithms for mining these dependencies and approximation techniques to handle dirty data.
core_content.key_methodology: Algorithms are adapted or modified to process nested JSON data efficiently. The first strategy flattens the input data, while the second adapts the algorithm specifically for JSON data processing.
outcomes: Defined new types of dependencies suitable for nested JSON data
Developed efficient algorithms for mining these dependencies on JSON documents
Implemented approximation techniques to handle dirty or incomplete data
core_content.full_text_markdown: Functional and inclusion dependencies are widely used in data profiling due to their ability to identify relationships such as primary and foreign keys. However, traditional definitions of these dependencies do not capture meaningful relationships involving nested data formats like JSON. This paper introduces new types of dependencies for nested JSON data and presents efficient algorithms for mining them. Two strategies are described: one that flattens the input data and uses existing algorithms, while another adapts the algorithm to process JSON data directly. The work also addresses dirty or incomplete data by implementing approximation techniques.
